items.title: Plane and ground vehicle collide at New York's LaGuardia Airport halting flights
items.summary: An Air Canada Express plane suffered significant damage after the collision.

items.title: Asia stocks slide as US and Iran threaten to escalate war
items.summary: The International Energy Agency chief said the war could lead to the world's worst energy crisis in decades.

items.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
items.summary: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

items.title: France's Socialists hold on to power in major cities in election boost for mainstream
items.summary: Paris and Marseille are held by the Socialists in local elections which saw the nationalist right win in Nice and certain towns.

items.title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
items.summary: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.

items.title: UN issues new climate warning as El Niño looms
items.summary: The Earth's climate is further out of balance than at any time in recorded history, the UN's weather agency says.

items.title: National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week
items.summary: Another total failure of its power grid underlines Cuba's hardship under a US-imposed fuel blockade.

items.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
items.summary: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

items.title: Trump says ICE agents will assist airport security as DHS shutdown continues
items.summary: Travellers have faced hours-long waits at airports around the country as a partial government shutdown has left TSA agents without pay.

items.title: Kenyans fighting illegally for Russia in Ukraine to be granted amnesty
items.summary: It is illegal for Kenyans to serve in foreign armies and can carry a sentence of up to 10 years in jail.

items.title: Hawaii storms have caused $1bn in damage, governor says
items.summary: The full scale of the damages to roads and other infrastructure from rain and flooding in the state must still be assessed, Gov Josh Green says.

items.title: 'We cannot be in peace', say Guthrie family as they renew appeal over missing mother
items.summary: A seven-week nationwide search for 84-year-old Nancy Guthrie, the mother of TV anchor Savannah Guthrie has not identified any suspects.

items.title: Sudan drone attack on key hospital killed 64 people during Eid, WHO says
items.summary: The strike killed 64 people, including 13 children, two nurses and a doctor, according to the WHO head.

items.title: Russian attacks kill six in Ukraine, officials say
items.summary: The deaths were reported in four south-eastern Ukrainian regions in the past 24 hours.

items.title: Qatari and Turkish citizens die in military helicopter crash
items.summary: Four Qatari military personnel, one Turkish serviceman and two technicians from a Turkish defence company were on board.

items.title: Robert Mueller, ex-FBI chief who led Trump-Russia investigation, dies at 81
items.summary: A former FBI director, Mueller led the high-profile inquiry into alleged Russian interference in the 2016 US election

items.title: The cutthroat battle for the US weight-loss drug market
items.summary: Weight-loss drug prices are falling in the US - but can the example be repeated?

items.title: Germany has a shortage of workers - so it's turning to India for help
items.summary: The European nation, struggling to find skilled staff, is giving jobs to young people from India.

items.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
items.summary: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

items.title: How a ban on religious symbols has triggered a Canadian constitutional debate
items.summary: Quebec's law to regulate religious symbols goes to the Supreme Court in a constitutional test case.

items.title: 'Peace is a gradual thing': How land, cattle and identity fuel a deadly Nigerian conflict
items.summary: How a lack of justice and trust in the security forces perpetuates deadly violence in Plateau state.

items.title: Italy is voting on whether to change its constitution. What does this mean for Meloni?
items.summary: The dry subject matter of this weekend's vote seems obscure but for many it is a vote on the PM.

items.title: Why is Israel targeting Lebanon's bridges?
items.summary: Israeli forces have been instructed to destroy the crossings over the Litani river that were being used by Hezbollah to send reinforcements.

items.title: 'A direct hit' - BBC visits Israeli town after Iranian strike
items.summary: The BBC's Sebastian Usher reports from Dimona in southern Israel, which was hit by an Iranian missile.

items.title: BBC in Cuba as island plunged into darkness
items.summary: More than 10 million people are enduring power cuts after Cuba's national electrical grid collapsed again.

items.title: 'BTS is everything for us': K-pop fans gather in Seoul for comeback show
items.summary: Some 260,000 fans are expected to watch BTS perform together on Saturday for the first time since 2022.
